column2: Back

- Salut, wei war dei Wiegennt?
- Ech war de ganze Wiegennt krank.
- Oh mei, wat has du dann?
- Ech mengen, ech hat Gripp.
- Seriö? An elo, as et schon rem besser?
- Jo, aschon rem bessen de Schnapp, mee et giet scho viel besser. A wie war dei Wiegennt?
- Ganz relax. E Samschdeg de Mettes, sinn ech bei mengem Tatair iesse gangen, an owes war ech mat menger Frëndin am Kino. An e Sonnden, sinn ech mat mengem Hond aus dem Asyl spazere gangen.
- Aso, mescht du dat oft?
- Jo jo, ech ginn regelméisseg an Deieren Asyl. Ech sinn ganz frou mat Muppen, mee ech hu scho keng Zäit a keng Plaz, fir en doheem ze hunn. Dofir ginn ech mat den Hënn aus dem Deieren Asyl spazere.
- Dat fannen ech super.
- Mojen, wéi geet et?
- Tip top. Ech war de ganze Wiegennt fort, an et war immens.
- Ah cool, wou was du dann?
- Am Italien. Ech hu vill vun der Uni besicht.
- Oh wéi flott, wéi laang hu mer eis net gesinn?
- Oh, ongeféier 10 Joer. A wéi war dei Wiegennt?
- Stressesch. Sonja war de Wiegennt fort, an ech war eleng mat de Kanner. An ech hat och nach vill Aarbecht.
- Oh, hues du de Wiegennt geschafft?
- Jo, ech hu 5 Tonnen an en Rapport fir den Direkter geschriwwen.
- Wirklech?
- Jo, ech hat dëse Wiegennt wierklech keng Zäit fir ze entspanen.
- Dat ass schued.
- Hello, how was your weekend?
- I was sick the whole weekend.
- Oh my, what happened to you then?
- I think I have the flu.
- Serious? And now, is it already a bit better?
- Yes, already a bit better, only the cough remains. And how was your weekend?
- Very relaxed. On Saturday the meet, I went to eat at my dad’s, and in the evening I was with my girlfriend at the cinema. And on Sunday, I walked with my dog from the animal shelter.
- So, do you do that often?
- Yes yes, I go regularly to the animal shelter. I am very happy with dogs, but I have no time and no place to have one at home. Therefore I go for walks with the dogs from the animal shelter.
- I think that’s great.
- Morning, how are you?
- Tip top. I was away the whole weekend, and it was enormous.
- Ah cool, where were you then?
- In Italy. I visited a lot of universities.
- Oh how nice, how long have we not seen each other?
- About 10 years. And how was your weekend?
- Stressful. Sonja was away the weekend, and I was alone with the kids. And I also had a lot of work.
- Oh, did you finish the weekend?
- Yes, I wrote 5 tons and a report for the director.
- Really?
- Yes, I really had no time to relax that weekend.
- That's a pity.

- Liest a lauscht dat den Dialog.
- So, den nächste Meindisch ass Feierdag?
- Jo, dann hu mer e lange Weekend.
- Dat ass super!
- Wat solle mer dann de Weekend maachen?
- Wesst du loscht, an de Müllerdaal ze fueren, an do en Trëppeltuert ze maachen?
- Dat hu mer schonn laang net mi gemaach, an d'Landschaft do ass wierklech immens.
- Jo, de Müllerdaal ass flott, mä ech hat awer éischter un eng aner Aktivitéit geduecht.
- Mir hu jo dräi Deeg Weekend.
- Mir kënne jo vläicht an d'Eislek fueren an och do iwernechten.
- Wat wëlls du dann am Eislek maachen?
- A wou wëlls du schlofen?
- Mä jo, mir kënne jo zum Beispill d'Dierstellung Family of Man zu Klierf kucke goen an d'Buerg zu Veianen.
- Mir kënne nach op d'Stauséi fueren.
- Do am Naturpark ginn et e ganze Koup flott Aktivitéiten.
- Iwernechte kënne mer op engem Camping oder an der Jugendherberg zu Lultz oder zu Veianen.
- Camping?
- Awer riicht, wat gëff dir das?
- Jo, we gët dir iwerhaapt de Weekend?
- Wann net heite Lëtzebuerg schéngt, kënne mer jo vläicht e klengen Ausfluch an eng Stad maachen.
- We wäret mat Paräis oder Prag?
- Oh jo, dat ass eng gutt Iddi.
- Komm, ma kucken emol d'Meteo, an dann decidéiere mer, wat mer maachen.
- Read and listen to the dialogue.
- So, is next Monday a holiday?
- Yes, then we have a long weekend.
- That's great!
- What should we do over the weekend?
- Do you want to go to Müllerthal and do a rock-climbing wall there?
- We haven't done that in a long time, and the landscape there is really immense.
- Yes, Müllerthal is nice, but I was thinking of a different activity.
- We have a three-day weekend after all.
- We could perhaps drive to the Eifel and stay there as well.
- What would you like to do in the Eifel?
- And where do you want to sleep?
- Well, we could go to see the Dierstellung Family of Man in Clervaux and the castle in Vianden.
- We could also go to the Sûre Valley.
- In the nature park there are a lot of nice activities.
- We could stay in a campsite or at the youth hostel in Lultzhausen or Vianden.
- Camping?
- But honestly, what are you thinking?
- So, what will you do mainly over the weekend?
- If not today, Luxembourg seems, could we perhaps take a short excursion to a city?
- How about Paris or Prague?
- Oh yes, that's a good idea.
- Come on, let's check the weather first, and then decide what we do.

- Ech will, dass mir zusammenbleiben an eis bestürzen.
- A mir kreie natürlich och Kanner, zwei oder drei an ein Kaas.
- Gut, an du schaffst da just nach 50% an die aner 50% kümmers du dich in Kanner.
- Äh, für was dann eisch?
- Du kannst jo doheem bleiwen oder Kanner ginn an Kriesch.
- Aha, awer twest du sus nach vier Pläng?
- Ech ging gerne d'Sprochentast machen an Lützebäuergin.
- Wann alles gut geht, da kann ich das schon den nächsten Sommer machen.
- An du noging ich mich auch nach gerne an der Informatik weiterbilden.
- A wat sind den Pläng?
- Hm, ech weiset nach net so genau.
- So, do you feel at home here in Dova, what do you want to do?
- Yes, I already have a concrete job position.
- I hope to stay with my company and build a career there.
- I want to earn enough money and buy a nice car with cash.
- Ah, you’re just thinking about your career.
- I’m mainly thinking about life.
- Yes, sure, that’s important too.
- I want us to stay together and settle down.
- And of course, we’ll have children, two or three, and a dog.
- Good, and you’ll work just 50%, and the other 50% you’ll take care of the kids.
- Uh, then what about you?
- You can stay at home or take care of the children in daycare.
- Aha, but do you still have other plans?
- I would like to take the language test in Luxembourgish.
- If all goes well, I can do that next summer.
- And I would also like to continue my education in computer science.
- And what are the plans?
- Hmm, I’m not exactly sure yet.

- Liest er lauschtert ein Interview.
- Guten Morgen, liebe neue Lauschterer.
- Willkommen an unserer Emission Dadasdleven.
- Haut zum Thema Migration.
- Als Gäst am Studio begrüßen wir Schaub Madame Bollich aus Bosnien, den Herr O'Donnell aus Irland und den Herr Sampaio aus Brasilien.
- Guten Morgen.
- Guten Morgen.
- Madame Bollich, dir kommt aus Bosnien.
- Wie lange lebt ihr schon zu Lützenbusch?
- Ich wohne schon seit 15 Jahren mit meiner Familie hier.
- Damals war ich krischer Bosnien, aber wir sind über Deutschland und Lützenbusch gekommen.
- Mit der ganzen Familie?
- Ich war allein mit den zwei Kanern.
- Mein Mann ist mir später nachgekommen.
- Als Drittkanzler sind sie zu Lützenbusch geboren.
- Das war bestimmt nicht einfach.
- Nein, am Anfang war es ganz schwer.
- Herr O'Donnell, wie war das bei Ihnen?
- Ich bin vor 20 Jahren nach Lützenbusch gekommen, um eine Formation zu erhalten.
- Ich bin Linguist.
- Wir haben da nicht mein Freund kennengelernt.
- Wir haben eine Zeit in Afrika gelebt.
- Aber wir sind schon seit ein paar Jahren nicht zu Lützenbusch.
- Und als Zeitchef hier?
- Ich geh Englisch kuren.
- Herr Sampaio, ihr wart noch ganz jung, als ihr nach Lützenbusch gekommen seid.
- Ja, ich hatte 19 Jahre, als ich an Europa gekommen bin.
- Ich war für Deutschland bei meiner Familie in Portugal und habe dort mein Freund kennengelernt.
- Mein Freund war aber nicht Portugies?
- Nein, sie ist Lützenbuscherin, aber war gerade in Portugal an der Vakanz.
- Nur eine Zeit haben wir entschieden, zusammen zu Lützenbusch zu leben.
- Das ist schon 10 Jahre her.
- Kommen wir zum Thema Sprache.
- Ihr schätzt allen drei super Lützenbuschisch.
- Wo habt ihr das so gut gelernt?
- Ich hatte so gutes Deutsch, als ich direkt an den Sprachkurs gegangen bin.
- Französisch kann ich nicht so gut, aber die Hilfe für meinen kann ich mir.
- Mein Freund ist Lützenbuscherin und ihr älterer schätzt nicht gut Englisch.
- Für mich war das Sprachlehren ein Mussesach, auch für mich heute zu integrieren, auch für meinen Kanner zu verstehen.
- Und ihr?
- Ich habe Lützenbusch an Oberkursen immer die Familie von meiner Frau gelehrt.
- Es war nicht leicht, aber ich war sehr immens motiviert.
- Und ihr braucht auch Lützenbusch wie ein Zukunftsplan?
- Für was?
- Das hat ihr immer noch über Tech-Musik.
- Das geht weiter an unserer Emission.
- Da das Leben.
- Heute zum Thema Migration.
- Listen to an interview.
- Good morning, dear new listeners.
- Welcome to our program Dadasdleven.
- Today on the topic of migration.
- As guests in the studio, we welcome Madame Bollich from Bosnia, Mr. O'Donnell from Ireland, and Mr. Sampaio from Brazil.
- Good morning.
- Good morning.
- Madame Bollich, you come from Bosnia.
- How long have you been living in Lützenbusch?
- I have been living here with my family for 15 years.
- Back then I was in war-torn Bosnia, but we came through Germany and Lützenbusch.
- With the whole family?
- I was alone with the two children.
- My husband joined me later.
- Mr. O'Donnell, you were born in Lützenbusch as a third-generation resident.
- That must not have been easy.
- No, at first it was very difficult.
- Mr. O'Donnell, how was it for you?
- I came to Lützenbusch 20 years ago to get training.
- I am a linguist.
- We met my friend there.
- We lived in Africa for a while.
- But we have been in Lützenbusch for a few years now.
- And as a time manager here?
- I take English courses.
- Mr. Sampaio, you were very young when you came to Lützenbusch.
- Yes, I was 19 years old when I came to Europe.
- I was in Germany with my family in Portugal and met my friend there.
- Your friend was not Portuguese?
- No, she is from Lützenbusch but was on vacation in Portugal.
- After some time, we decided to live together in Lützenbusch.
- That was already 10 years ago.
- Let's move on to the topic of language.
- All three of you speak excellent Lützenbuschisch.
- Where did you learn it so well?
- I already had good German when I went straight to the language course.
- I don't speak French very well, but I can manage with help for my child.
- My friend is from Lützenbusch and her older child doesn't speak English well.
- For me, learning the language was a must, also to integrate today and to understand my child.
- And you?
- I have always taught my wife's family Lützenbusch in advanced courses.
- It was not easy, but I was very motivated.
- And do you also need Lützenbusch for your future plans?
- For what?
- That is still about tech music.
- That continues on our program.
- That's life.
- Today on the topic of migration.

- Liest allar d'Texter vun dënen zwee tierdüscht Land.
- Lëtzebuerg, Land vun de Schlässer an de Burschen.
- Burschen, Schlässer an Festungsruine gehéieren zu de wichtegsten Attraktionen vun Lëtzebuerg.
- Et ginn am ganze 76 Burschen a Schlässer oder ob Manns Truiden dofon.
- Firun e puer Joerzéngten huet de Staat ugefaangen, déi wichtegst Gebaier opzekafen, aser fir Touristen aus dem In- an Ausland zougänglech ze machen.
- Die schönst renoviert Schlässer stin zu Feianen, Befort, Burschent, Klef an an der Fels.
- Die flott Ruine kanen zu Didling, Dürlem, Ersauer, Hesper, Kersch, Mersch an Usselding bewonneren.
- Et gefiel kulturell Aktivitäten an dese Burschen a Schlässer.
- Konseren, Kunstausstellungen, Konferenzen, Musikkfestivallen an Mittelalterfester.
- Am bekanntste sie wohl Fotoausstellung Family of Man vum Edward Steichen am Schloss zu Klef an Mittelalterfest, dat immer am Sommer am Schloss zu Feianen ass.
- Leidt, die gewanderigien, können den nationalen Trippeltour vom Dall von de Siebeschlässer machen.
- Dat isse Wee von 37 Kilometer, die von Geischel op der Belger Grenz bis op Mersch geht.
- Den Tour geht durch Böscher, Felder an Dierfer, immer den Floss eisch no.
- An ennerwee können die weit Schlässer von Kärsch, Zimmer, Ernseburg, Heisender 2, Hölmes, Schöndes an Mersch.
- Read all the texts from these two Germany-language countries.
- Luxembourg, country of the castles and the boys.
- Boys, castles and fortress ruins belong to the most important attractions of Luxembourg.
- There are in total 76 castles and fortresses or on Manns Truiden there of them.
- For a few decades the state has started to buy up the most important buildings, in order to make them accessible to tourists from home and abroad.
- The most beautifully renovated castles are in Feianen, Befort, Burschent, Klef and in the rock.
- The beautiful ruins can be admired in Didling, Dürlem, Ersauer, Hesper, Kersch, Mersch and Usselding.
- There are cultural activities in these castles and fortresses.
- Concerts, art exhibitions, conferences, music festivals and medieval festivals.
- The best known is probably the Family of Man photograph exhibit by Edward Steichen at the castle in Klef and the medieval festival, which always takes place in the summer at the castle in Feianen.
- People, who wander, can do the national triple-town tour from the valley of the Siebeschlässer.
- This is the route of 37 kilometers, from Geischel at the Belger border to Mersch.
- The tour goes through Böscher, Felder and Dierfer, always following the river.
- And in the end, you can see far castles of Kärsch, Zimmer, Ernseburg, Heisender 2, Hölmes, Schöndes and Mersch.

- The best known is probably the Family of Man photograph exhibit by Edward Steichen at the castle in Klef and the medieval festival, which always takes place in the summer at the castle in Feianen.
- People, who wander, can do the national triple-town tour from the valley of the Siebeschlässer.
- This is the route of 37 kilometers, from Geischel at the Belger border to Mersch.
- The tour goes through Böscher, Felder and Dierfer, always following the river.
- And in the end, you can see far castles of Kärsch, Zimmer, Ernseburg, Heisender 2, Hölmes, Schöndes and Mersch.

- Löschtert Kommentaren iwwer zwou Kansungen.
- Wat hir Kommentar passt bei wat hir Foto?
- Schreibt zu all der Runden an notaréiert 3 duerch 4 bei all Foto.
- 1. Hüt, dat war ganz iwwerall.
- Mir waren all doer op der Pist, a mir hunn och endlech Snowboard geléiert.
- De Schnéi war super.
- De Chalet och.
- Mir, et waren ze vill Leit bei de Liften.
- 2. Eis Vakanz war einfach super.
- Et huet vill gereent, mee et war ni langweileg.
- Mir hunn vill Leit kennegeléiert, a nach ouni Eisfrënn war et ganz flott.
- Ech sinn awer frou, erëm am mengem Bett ze schlofen.
- 3. Wat en Erliefnes.
- Et waren esou vill wäit Déieren ze gesinn, et war einfach emolisch.
- Mir sinn all doer erausgefuer, an och en Kayak gemaach.
- Dat war immens beandrockend, an ech hu bësse gefaart.
- 4. Et war schéin, d’ganz Famill erëm ze gesinn.
- Ech hat se schonn laang net méi gesinn.
- Mir waren vum Moies bis owes do, och vill giess, vill erzielt, a vill gelacht.
- 5. Dat war wierklech dat Standardprogramm fir eng verléift Koppel.
- Mir sinn schick erausgaangen, sinn mam Bus iwwer 10 gefuer, an hunn eng Tour iwwer de Montmartre gemaach.
- Och mam Wieder haten mir Chance, d'Sonn huet de ganze Weekend geschéngt.
- 6. Wat haten mir eng schéi zweet Halschent.
- De Kaddo vun de Kanner war wierklech immens.
- Mir hunn all doer gutt giess, de Summer war immens gemittlech, mir hunn eis richteg gutt erholl, a kënne lo nächst voller Energie op d’Kanner oppassen.
- Delete comments about two vacations.

- 1. Today, that was quite everywhere.
- We were all there on the slopes, and we also finally learned snowboarding.
- The snow was great.
- The chalet too.
- We, there were too many people at the lifts.
- 2. Our vacation was simply great.
- It rained a lot, but it was never boring.
- We met many people, and even without our friends it was very nice.
- I am, however, glad to sleep again in my bed.
- 3. What an experience.
- There were so many animals to see, it was just amazing.
- We all went out, and also did a kayak.
- That was incredibly impressive, and I was a little scared.
- 4. It was nice to see the whole family again.
- I hadn’t seen them in a long time.
- We were there from morning till night, ate a lot, told a lot, and laughed a lot.
- 5. That was really the standard program for a couple in love.
- We dressed up, rode the bus for over 10, and did a tour of Montmartre.
- Even with the weather we had a chance, the sun shone the entire weekend.
- 6. What a beautiful second half we had.
- The children’s gift was really enormous.
- We all ate well there, the summer was very cozy, we really rested well, and can next go full of energy look after the kids.

- An, wéi war e Vakanz?
- Super, virde war zwar net genial, mir mieren awer immens vill gemach.
- Jo, wat hutt dir da gemach?
- Mir sinn oft an den Zentrum gefuer fir shoppen ze goen, a mir sinn och op Pormüse gekuckt gaangen.
- A wann virde gutt war, sinn mir op Plasch gaangen.
- Hutt dir vill Leit kennengeléiert?
- Jo, mir hu vill Leit um Camping getraff, an e Pär Franzosen op der Visite vun der Stad.
- Si ware ganz sympathesch, a mir sinn no der Visite zesumme eppes drénken gaangen.
- A wat hutt dir haut owes gemach?
- Owes hu mir mat de Leit um Camping getschillt, oder mir sinn an d'Disco gaangen.
- Flott, hues du haut Fotoen gemaach?
- Jo, natierlech, wëlls du se kucken?
- So, how was your vacation?
- Great, the trip wasn’t terrible, but we got a lot done.
- Yes, what did you do there?
- We went often to the city center to go shopping, and we also looked at Pormüse.
- And when good, we went to a place.
- did you meet many people?
- Yes, we met many people at the campground, and a pair of French people on a city tour.
- They were very friendly, and after the tour we went for a drink together.
- And what did you do this evening?
- Tonight we hung out with the people at the campground, or we went to the disco.
- Great, did you take photos today?
- Yes, of course, would you like to see them?

- Wat ass geschitt?
- Hallo, wien ass do?
- Ech sinn et, de Paul.
- Wéi geet et dir?
- Et geet mir net gutt.
- Wat ass geschitt?
- Ech sinn gefall an hunn mir de Been gebrach.
- Dat ass schlëmm! Wou bass du?
- Ech sinn am Spidol.
- Brauchs du Hëllef?
- Jo, ech brauch eppes ze drénken.
- Ech kommen direkt.
- Merci villmools.
- Gär geschitt.
- Bis geschwënn!
- Bis dann!
- What happened?
- Hello, who is there?
- I am him, Paul.
- How are you?
- I’m not feeling well.
- What happened?
- I fell and broke my leg.
- That’s bad! Where are you?
- I am in the hospital.
- Do you need help?
- Yes, I need something to drink.
- I am coming right away.
- Thank you very much.
- Glad it happened.
- See you soon!
- See you!

- Moie Paul, ech sinn et.
- So, ech mengen, ech hu mäi Portofeuil verluer.
- Kanns du kucken, op en doheem ass?
- Hei ass en net.
- Oh nee!
- Vu wounnersch en dann nëmme verluer?
- Dann iwerlee emol.
- Wou wärs du haut iwwerall?
- Ech sinn de Moie wéi ëmmer schaffe gefuer.
- Ech hunn de Bus geholl, mee ech hunn meng Kaar net gewisen.
- An um Büro hues du de Portofeuil och net gebraucht?
- Nee, an an der Mëttespaus och net.
- Ech si mam Claudia iesse gaangen, hatt huet mech invitéiert.
- An duerno?
- Duerno hat ech e Rendezvous beim Dokter.
- Ech hunn d'Rechnung net direkt bezuelt, mä si matgeholl.
- A wou bass du elo?
- Ech sinn an engem Kleederbuttik an ech hu vill flott Kleeder ugebuess.
- Mä an der Keess hunn ech mäi Portofeuil gesicht an en net fonnt.
- Hmm, dat deet mer leed.
- Da muss du elo ouni nei Kleeder heemkommen.
- Hello Paul, I am it.
- So, I think I have lost my wallet.
- Can you look, is it at home?
- Here is one not.
- Oh no!
- From where did you lose it then somewhere?
- Then I will think again.
- Where would you be today everywhere?
- I am the morning as always gone to work.
- I took the bus, but I did not show my card.
- And in the office did you not need the wallet either?
- No, and in the lunch break also not.
- I went to eat with Claudia, she invited me.
- And afterwards?
- Afterwards I had a date with the doctor.
- I did not pay the bill directly, but it was included.
- And where are you now?
- I am in a clothing store and I offered a lot of nice clothes.
- But in the checkout I searched my wallet and did not find it.
- Hmm, that hurts me.
- Then you have to come home now without new clothes.

- Salut, wéi war däi Weekend?
- Ech war de ganze Weekend krank.
- Oh mei, wat has du dann?
- Ech mengen, ech hat Gripp.
- Seriö? An elo, ass et schon erëm besser?
- Jo, ech schnëppen nach e bëssen, mee et geet scho vill besser.
- A wéi war däi Weekend?
- Ganz relax, e Samschdes de Mëtteg sinn ech bei meng Tata iesse gaangen, an owes war ech mat enger Frëndin an de Kino.
- A e Sonndes sinn ech mat engem Hond aus dem Asyl spadséiere gaangen.
- Also, méchs du dat méi oft?
- Jo, jo, ech gi reegelméisseg an d'Déierenasyl.
- Ech si ganz frou mat Muppen, mee ech hu keng Zäit a keng Plaz, fir een doheem ze hunn.
- Dofir gi ech mat den Hënn aus dem Déierenasyl spadséieren.
- Dat fannen ech super.
- Hello, how was your weekend?
- I was sick all weekend.
- Oh my, what happened then?
- I think I had the flu.
- Seriously? And now, is it better again?
- Yes, I’m still a little tired, but it’s already much better.
- And how was your weekend?
- Very relaxed, on Saturday around noon I went to eat at my aunt's, and in the evening I was with a friend at the cinema.
- And Sunday I went for a walk with a dog from the shelter.
- So, do you do that more often?
- Yes, yes, I regularly go to the animal shelter.
- I am very fond of dogs, but I have no time and no place to keep one at home.
- Therefore I go for walks with the dogs from the animal shelter.
- I think that’s great.
